entry.title: Maximizing M&A Success: How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness
entry.content:
Maximizing M&A Success:  How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness

Authored by:  Christina Grace, SAP Global Finance Value Advisor & Marie Trojani, SAP Global HCM Value Advisor

Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) are complex transactions that can significantly alter the trajectory of a business. While they present opportunities for growth, diversification, and enhanced market presence, they also come with inherent challenges that can hinder success. One critical factor in navigating these challenges is the effective integration of systems, processes, people, and cultures. SAP offers a robust framework that can significantly enhance a company’s effectiveness for companies pursuing an M&A strategy. This blog explores how SAP facilitates successful M&A outcomes in the areas of Finance and HR and discusses potential inflection points in the journey to ensure success.

Understanding the M&A Landscape

Before delving into how SAP can assist during the M&A process, it's important to understand the typical M&A landscape. The process generally involves several phases:

  1. Planning and Strategy Development: Identifying potential targets and assessing strategic fit. This includes an assessment of current systems, people, and processes in both organizations. The goal should be to identify gaps and overlaps that could impact integration.  This phase also involves engaging key stakeholders from both organizations to ensure buy-in and prepare teams for the upcoming changes.
  2. Due Diligence: Analyzing financials, operations, and risks associated with the target company.  This analysis should provide insights into the target company’s strengths and weaknesses.  During this phase, data management tools can be used to perform scenario modeling of the two companies, set KPI goals, measure compliance, and ensure data harmonization.  A risk assessment should be performed to evaluate potential risks associated with the acquisition. Identifying risks early allows for proactive management strategies. That’s also at this stage that you can evaluate the cultural fit between the two companies.
  3. Integration and Strategy Management: Merging systems, processes, people, and cultures post-acquisition. Develop a comprehensive integration roadmap that outlines timelines, responsibilities, and key milestones. This roadmap should leverage GenAI integration capabilities to ensure a seamless transition. Prepare a training and support plan to educate employees about new systems and processes. Effective training will minimize disruptions and foster a sense of ownership among staff.  Real-time monitoring of the integration process is essential and allows for quick adjustments if any issues arise.  Establish feedback mechanisms to gather input from employees about the integration process. Utilize these insights to refine strategies and address concerns promptly. Particularly, HR concerns can appear quickly around job redundancies, salary gaps, talent retention for critical roles and employee morale.
  4. Post-Merger Evaluation: Assessing the success of the integration and overall M&A performance. After integration, evaluate the performance of the combined entity using advanced analytics tools. Assess whether M&A goals have been met and identify areas for further improvement, realizing greater synergies, and operating more efficiently.  Perform a cultural integration review by assessing employee engagement and satisfaction.  This allows companies to gauge the success of cultural integration, to gather insights, and to address any lingering issues.

Each phase presents unique challenges that SAP addresses with agility, transparency, and scalability.

The Role of SAP in M&A

SAP provides a comprehensive suite of integrated solutions designed to enhance operational efficiency, drive revenue growth, harmonize data, and improve real-time insights and overall business intelligence. Here’s how SAP Finance and HR solutions can specifically help companies pursuing an M&A strategy:

Seamless Integration of Systems

Challenge: One of the biggest hurdles in M&A is integrating disparate IT systems from merging organizations. Incompatible systems can lead to inefficiencies, data silos, and disruptions in business operations.

SAP Solution: SAP’s integrated platform enables organizations to unify disparate systems under one umbrella. With tools such as SAP S/4HANA, businesses can achieve real-time data integration and consistency, allowing for streamlined operations across the newly formed entity.  In the HR community, harmonizing different payroll, benefits, and performance management systems requires strategic planning and meticulous execution to avoid disruptions and maintain employee satisfaction.  This seamless integration reduces downtime and ensures continuity of service, which is critical during transitional phases.

Efficient Data Management

Challenge: During an M&A integration, organizations face an overwhelming amount of data that needs to be migrated, analyzed, and integrated. Poor data management can result in costly mistakes and compliance issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s data management tools, including SAP Business Technology Platform, provide robust capabilities for data migration, cleansing, and integration. These solutions ensure that data from both companies are harmonized and accessible, allowing decision-makers to derive insights quickly and effectively. Real-time analytics further enable businesses to identify trends and make informed decisions swiftly.

Streamlined Financial Reporting

Challenge: Accurate financial reporting is essential during M&A, both for compliance and for assessing the financial health of the combined entity. However, multiple financial systems and reporting standards can complicate this process.

SAP Solution: SAP solutions offer integrated financial management tools that facilitate streamlined reporting and compliance with international financial standards. With SAP Analytics Cloud, businesses can create real-time financial dashboards, enabling stakeholders to monitor KPIs and other financial metrics seamlessly. This capability is crucial for transparent communication with investors, internal stakeholders, and regulatory bodies.

Enhanced Operational Efficiency

Challenge: Post-M&A, organizations often encounter operational redundancies and inefficiencies due to overlapping processes and systems.  Furthermore, redundancies and restructuring can lead to workforce reductions, which may result in the loss of valuable talent and institutional knowledge.

SAP Solution: SAP’s process automation capabilities can help identify and eliminate redundancies. For example, with SAP Ariba, companies can enhance procurement processes, while SAP SuccessFactors can streamline human resources management. HR must manage these transitions delicately, ensuring fair and compliant processes while maintaining operational continuity. These solutions foster operational efficiencies that lead to cost savings and improved productivity across the organization. 

Change Management Support

Challenge: M&A transactions often lead to significant challenges to company culture, processes, and personnel. Concerns about the unknown and resistance to change can derail integration efforts. 

SAP Solution:  Fostering employee engagement and loyalty can be key to overcoming these obstacles. Initiatives to support change management, such as training programs and team-building activities, are essential but must be carefully tailored to address the specific needs and concerns of the workforce.  SAP offers tools to support change management, including SAP Enable Now, which provides training and support resources for employees.  This tool allows organizations to create custom training materials and resources that can facilitate a smoother transition. By preparing employees for the changes ahead, companies can mitigate resistance and foster a more collaborative environment.

Risk Management and Compliance

Challenge: M&A transactions are inherently risky, and the complexities of integrating different compliance standards can lead to potential legal and regulatory issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s risk management tools, such as SAP GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance), enable businesses to assess and manage risks effectively. These solutions provide a framework for identifying compliance gaps and ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements, thereby protecting the organization from potential liabilities.

Cultural Integration

Challenge: Merging different company cultures can be one of the most challenging aspects of M&A. Misalignment can lead to employee dissatisfaction and turnover.  M&A often herald significant organizational transformation, presenting numerous challenges for HR and employees. Merging distinct corporate cultures can lead to misalignment, confusion, and conflicts. HR must navigate the complex task of creating a cohesive environment where employees from both organizations feel valued and understood.

SAP Solution: While SAP solutions primarily focus on operational aspects, they can also support cultural integration indirectly. Tools like SAP SuccessFactors provide insights into employee engagement and performance, enabling leadership to address cultural challenges proactively. By fostering open communication and collaboration through these platforms, organizations can work towards aligning their cultures more effectively.

Job Security

Challenge: Another critical issue is job security. Employees often face anxiety and uncertainty about their future roles, leading to decreased morale and productivity. There is a higher risk for top talent to leave the company during times of uncertainty.

SAP Solution: Transparent communication from HR is vital to mitigate these concerns, as well as a comprehensive view into employee data such as competencies and job profiles. Analytics and AI can facilitate informed decisions and proactively alert if there are potential risks. Yet finding the balance between transparency and confidentiality can be challenging.

In navigating all of these challenges, Finance, HR and IT all play a crucial role in facilitating a smooth transition, fostering a positive organizational culture, and maintaining employee well-being and productivity during M&A activities.

Benefits of SAP Solutions in M&A

  • Efficiency: Streamlines operations and reduces integration time
  • Cost Savings: Minimizes redundancies and operational costs
  • Real-Time Insights: Provides data-driven decision-making capabilities
  • Risk Mitigation: Enhances compliance and governance
  • Employee Engagement: Supports smooth cultural integration through training and engagement tools

Conclusion

In today’s fast-paced business environment, the success of M&A hinges on effective integration and management of systems, people, data, and processes. In an era where adaptability and efficiency are paramount, embracing SAP Finance and HR solutions not only supports immediate M&A goals but also lays a solid foundation for sustained success in an ever-evolving marketplace.

SAP offers a comprehensive toolkit for companies navigating the multifaceted complexities and challenges of M&A, enabling organizations to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and drive overall success.  By leveraging SAP’s integrated tools, companies can navigate the complexities of M&A with greater confidence and effectiveness. By facilitating integration, enhancing data management, and supporting operational efficiency, SAP helps organizations achieve their M&A objectives while laying the groundwork for long-term success.  As organizations continue to pursue growth through acquisitions, those that adopt SAP solutions will be well-positioned to realize the full potential of their M&A strategies, fostering a more agile and competitive future.

entry.title: 📢 Transition from TS410 to Cloud-Based IE2EE
entry.content:
SAP University Alliances member institutions have long offered SAP certification to highlight students' SAP expertise. The "Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA (TS410)" course will retire on June 30, 2025 and be replaced by "End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise (IE2EE)" with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, which is now available. SAP University Alliances will provide next steps for users of TS410. 

📢 For new certifications, start with the cloud-based course

entry.title: Um Evento que Encheu os Olhos da Comunidade SAP em Recife #SITREC2024
entry.content:
No último dia 5 de outubro, Recife recebeu de braços abertos mais uma edição do SAP Inside Track, que não acontecia desde 2020. Esse evento, que já é uma tradição para a comunidade SAP no Brasil desde 2009, chegou à sua 43ª edição cercado de grande expectativa. E o público, formado por profissionais da área em Recife, respondeu à altura: os 100 ingressos destinados ao público geral esgotaram-se menos de um mês antes do evento.

O SIT Recife 2024 aconteceu nas dependências da Uninassau, que nos acolheu com muita atenção e cordialidade, tivemos o patrocínio da MakeValue, GFX, Tachyonix e Accenture. E também com o apoio da Moovi Education. Cerca de 150 participantes estiveram presentes, e o sucesso foi tanto que já começamos a planejar o próximo encontro. A empolgação da comunidade nos faz refletir sobre os pontos a melhorar para as próximas edições, mas também nos motiva a continuar promovendo essa festa da comunidade SAP na cidade de Recife.

Logo na abertura, fomos brindados com uma apresentação de uma orquestra de frevo, a tradicional dança de Recife, conhecida nacional e internacionalmente. Ao som de frevos clássicos, dois passistas animaram o público com suas acrobacias enquanto os participantes desfrutavam de um café da manhã caprichado. A festa ficou ainda mais tecnológica com a presença de um robô da Accenture, um dos patrocinadores do evento, que também arriscou alguns passos de frevo!

Além das palestras, o evento foi um momento especial para reencontros. Profissionais da comunidade SAP, muitos dos quais só conhecíamos virtualmente, tiveram a chance de se encontrar pessoalmente, trocar experiências e tirar muitas fotos para registrar esses momentos. Foi incrível ver o networking acontecendo de forma tão natural.

O evento contou com a presença de @marco_v_santos , que abriu o evento falando sobre as inovações na plataforma da comunidade SAP, destacando como essas mudanças facilitam a interação entre profissionais em suas respectivas localidades. Ele também aproveitou para divulgar o grupo da comunidade SAP de Recife, criado para que todos possam compartilhar blogs, discussões e novidades sobre o mundo SAP.

Os organizadores desta edição @Marssel700 , ao lado de @RobertaFernande , Helder Lins e @afigueredo54, e também o palestrante João Otto demos boas-vindas aos participantes. Além disso, tivemos a Cláudia Danielle como mestre de cerimônia, conduzindo as atividades de palco com muita habilidade e simpatia.

As palestras mantiveram o público atento até o fim. Em eventos desse porte, é comum que o número de participantes diminua ao longo do dia, já que muitos escolhem assistir apenas a palestras específicas. No entanto, nesta edição, o interesse foi tanto que a maioria do público permaneceu até o último minuto, e na foto final, o auditório estava cheio! Isso demonstra o quanto a comunidade estava engajada e ávida por conhecimento.

A agenda do evento foi diversificada, cobrindo temas de grande relevância para o mercado SAP. Confira abaixo a lista das palestras que tivemos:

 

Palestrante - Link da Apresentação gravada no Youtube:

✔️ Key Note - Credenciamento e abertura
✔️ Marssel Vilaça - Maximizando o Valor do S/4HANA
✔️ Jonys Arcanjo - Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence: Revolucionando a Gestão de Crédito
✔️ Lucas Guedes Ferreira - Clean Core, SAP BTP e como a Tachyonix é a peça que faltava nesse quebra-cabeça
✔️Manuel Robalinho - Business innovation challenges and impacts for S/4HANA
✔️Antonio Figueredo e João Paulo Cabral - Facilita com BTP no Grupo Cornélio Brennand
✔️Frederico Gurgel - GFX Pagamento com cartões integrado ao S/4HANA Public Edition
✔️Danilo C. Bispo - Desmitificando o SAP DRC NFe - Como se preparar para uma migração?
✔️João Otto Gethmann Jr e Alan Dias - Inovação em SAP

Ainda temos os Podcasts com palestrantes do SITREC2024

🎙️Episódio 01 - Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais

🎙️Episódio 02 - Live Track: Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence para Gestão de Crédito

Como é tradição, o evento foi encerrado com sorteios de brindes. Entre os itens sorteados estavam livros da SAP Press, cursos da Moovi Education e equipamentos eletrônicos da MakeValue. Foi um final empolgante para um dia que certamente ficará na memória de todos os presentes.

Essa foi a quarta edição do SAP Inside Track em Recife, e o evento só confirma o quanto o mercado SAP cresce na região, com a chegada de novos parceiros e a capacitação de grandes profissionais para implementar as tecnologias mais recentes. Mal podemos esperar pela próxima edição, que já está nos planos para 2024.

 Todas as Fotos (All Photos)

Enquanto isso, fique de olho nos eventos Warm up SITFOR, SIT Fortaleza e SIT Curitiba, que estão a todo vapor! Ainda dá tempo de participar dessa grande confraternização da comunidade SAP, cheia de aprendizado e networking. Não deixe de seguir os links abaixo para ficar por dentro de tudo o que acontece no mundo SAP aqui no Brasil.

Sigam as Páginas:

SIT Brazil

https://www.linkedin.com/in/sitbrazil/

https://www.youtube.com/@SAPInsideTrack

SIT Fortaleza

https://www.linkedin.com/groups/12467481/

Fortaleza - SAP Community

https://www.youtube.com/@sitfor-sapinsidetrackforta6071

SIT Curitiba

https://www.linkedin.com/events/sapinsidetrackcuritiba20247244806570096640002/

Curitiba - SAP Community

SIT São Paulo

https://www.linkedin.com/in/sap-inside-track-s%C3%A3o-paulo-a43b99305/

São Paulo - SAP Community

 

 

Agradecemos a todos que puderam participar e aqueles que não puderam, não percam os próximos eventos. Sigam o nosso grupo para ficarem por dentro de todas as novidades. Abraço a todos!

entry.title: Tech Day: dive into the latest innovations in SAP BTP, SAC, Datasphere and more
entry.content:
Who are we?

We are a group of motivated developers and data experts, passionate about SAP, BTP, analytics and developing awesome applications! With this event we want to bring together people with a similar mindset, looking to explore SAP’s latest offerings and gain new insights.

 

What to expect?

  • Arrive at 12:30 PM for lunch and meet your fellow attendees.
  • At 1:30 PM we’ll kick off the afternoon with a keynote that will inspire you for the rest of the program.
  • After the keynote, we’ll split up in different tracks: Analytics & Integration, Extensions, and a Trending track. Have a look at the complete program here.
  • At 3:00 PM we'll hold a sweet break where you can talk with the experts.
  • At around 5:45 PM we'll call it a day, grab a drink (you can even participate in a beer tasting) and a bite and start connecting.

 

Practical information

Date: November 7, from 12:30 till 19:30

Location: Palm Brewery (De Hoorn) | Steenhuffeldorp 3, 1840 Londerzeel

 

Who can attend?

Flexso Tech Day is free to attend for every SAP developer and BI expert looking to stay up to date with the latest SAP technologies and network with like-minded spirits. Places are limited though, so register now and be sure of your spot!

 

We hope to see you there!

entry.title: Procedure For End-To-End Encryption And Signing Of File Through SFTP Via Integration Suite PART-2
entry.content:
I have a requirement where I must process a payment file generated in SAP through integration suite by encrypting and signing the file, sending the file to the bank server. We are sending and receiving the payment file through SFTP, to connect to the bank server, we have provided them with a PUBLIC SSH KEY. We were given the public key of the receiver, folder path to place the file, server address and credentials of the same to login and we must process the file generated in SAP to the receiver by encrypting and signing the file. We should encrypt the data using receiver’s public key and sign the same using our private key. I have discussed the keys part in my first blog in this blog i would like to share the details regarding integration flow part, if any one have missed any details regarding the first part I am sharing the link here for your access to the first blog,

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/procedure-for-end-to-end-encryption-and-signing-of-file-through-sftp-via/ba-p/13873725

Once you have successfully uploaded the keys in PGP keys and after testing the connectivity of source and target destinations, we now have configured the sender and receiver configuration, I have developed my integration flow, please find below image,

 

I had captured the file which came from SAP in the content modifier and now I have the necessary data to be encrypted and signed, let us now see the configuration of PGPEncryptor, In PGPEncryptor please go to processing and there you can find the first option where is says signatures, as shown below,

 

 

  • I must encrypt the data and sign the same, so I am including the signatures.
  • You can select the content encryption algorithm, secret key length and compression algorithm according to your requirement, I had changed the compression algorithm to uncompressed as I don’t want to compress the data.
  • There is an option of armored, if you have enabled/ticked/checked the box then the resulting PGP message will be encoded with a specific header in base64.
  • You can check the integrity protected data packet if you want to create a symmetrically encrypted and integrity protected data packet.
  • Now you can find the option of encryption key user IDs from public keyring where you must add public key of the receiver which we have earlier uploaded in PGP Keys, also find the signer key user IDs where we must add our private/secret key which we have generated using GPA tool earlier and uploaded to the PGP keys, please find the image below,

 

 

Now that you have done with configuration of encryption and signing, let me also show you the configuration for decryption as shown below,

 

 

  • In PGPDecryptor there is no configuration for the decryption unless you want to verify the signature, where you receive the data which is encrypted and signed.
  • As you are receiver in the case of decryptor you require the public key of the sender to verify the signature of the sender.

I would like to share my point that this requirement is configured for host to host configuration with bank and customer.

So, I conclude the whole requirement where we have seen generating SSH KEYS using PUTTY KEY GENERATOR, generating KEY PAIR using GPA TOOL, processing the payment file from SAP to BANK server by encrypting and signing the file.

Please provide me your valuable feedback and if you any queries feel free to share.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 3 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, October 11th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, October 14th.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

If you are looking for Week 1 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 2 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities - SAP Community

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the second week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here later in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  This week's Fun Friday activity will be 🟡 Bugtoberfest 2024 - SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Schedule Publication API's: Getting Started Guide
entry.content:
Introduction to the Schedule Publication API

The Schedule Publication API is designed to facilitate the seamless integration of scheduling functionalities for selected reports within the SAP Analytics Cloud. This API enables partners and customers to programmatically manage the publication of reports, ensuring that all actions typically performed through the user interface can also be executed via API calls.

This API is particularly beneficial for customers seeking event-based scheduling, as it allows for the creation and triggering of publication schedules in response to specific events. By leveraging the capabilities of the Schedule Publication API, users can automate workflows, enhance operational efficiency, and ensure the timely delivery of critical reports. Whether it's initiating a schedule based on an external trigger or managing report publications on a recurring basis, this API provides the flexibility and control needed to streamline the reporting process.

This is available from QRC4.2024 and in fast track tenant it is available from Version 2024.21

API Guide

API Description Method Endpoint Usage

 

APIDescriptionMethodEndPointUsage
Template APIRetrieves a prefilled JSON payload for creating, updating, or deleting schedules.GET/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/templateGet this template, then modify the placeholder values based on the requirement and use it for Creating or updating the Schedule.
Create APIEstablish a new single or recurring Schedule Publication.POST/api/v1/storyPublication/schedulesSend your modified JSON payload from the template to create a schedule.
Update APIModify specific properties of an existing Schedule Publication.PATCH/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/scheduleIDMake modifications without needing to recreate the entire schedule.
Delete APIRemove a single schedule or a series of schedules.DELETE/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/scheduleIDClean up outdated or unnecessary schedules easily.
Get Schedule APIRetrieve details of a specific Schedule Publication by its ID.GET/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/scheduleIDFetch the details of the specified schedule.

Note: Replace scheduleID with the ID of the schedule you want to update, delete, or retrieve.

API Body Payload Definition

The following payload will be returned by the template API:

 

 

 

 

{
    "storyId": "",
    "scheduleName": "",
    "recurrenceType": 1,
    "runNow": true,
    "scheduleMode": 0,
    "runOnceStartDate": "2024-10-14T15:02:56.111Z",
    "recurrence": [
        {
            "recurrenceMode": 3,
            "endMode": {
                "endModeRepetitions": 2,
                "endModeDate": null
            },
            "onMonday": false,
            "onTuesday": false,
            "onWednesday": false,
            "onThursday": false,
            "onFriday": false,
            "onSaturday": false,
            "onSunday": false,
            "firstRun": "2024-10-14T15:02:56.111Z",
            "dayCount": 30
        }
    ],
    "addStoryLink": true,
    "fileFormat": "PDF",
    "emailSubject": "",
    "emailMessage": "",
    "deliveryOption": 0,
    "timezoneFullName": "",
    "views": [
        {
            "bookmarkId": "STORY",
            "viewName": "view 1",
            "filename": "Sample",
            "fileSettings": {
                "selectedStoryPages": [],
                "includeAppendix": true,
                "appendixIncludeDate": true,
                "resolution": "1920,1080",
                "includeComments": false,
                "pageSize": "Auto"
            },
            "destinations": {
                "email": {
                    "extEmailUsr": [],
                    "internalEmailUsr": []
                }
            }
        }
    ],
    "xsTimeTaken": 53
}

 

 

 

 

Field Definitions

  • storyId: (string) Unique identifier for the story.
  • scheduleName: (string) Name of the schedule.
  • recurrenceType: (integer) Type of recurrence; defines how often the schedule runs. For a single schedule enter 1;  for a recurring schedule enter 2.
  • runNow: (boolean) Indicates whether the schedule should run immediately.
  • scheduleMode: (integer) Mode of the schedule. For broadcasting enter 1, for bursting enter 2
  • runOnceStartDate: (string) Specifiy UTC Date String for start date (ex:'2022-06-07T08:15:46.523Z').
  • recurrence: (array) List of recurrence settings.
    • recurrenceMode: (integer) Mode of recurrence. For hourly recurrence enter 2; for daily recurrence enter 3; for weekly recurrence enter 4 (to specify the day of the week, use true/false operators (ex: onMonday: true); for monthly recurrence enter 5
    • endMode: (object) Settings for ending the recurrence. You can use either endModeRepetitions or endModeDate. Specify the number of times you want the schedule to repeat using the corresponding number (ex: 2, for 2 repetitions of the schedule
      • endModeRepetitions: (integer) Number of repetitions before ending.
      • endModeDate: (string|null) End date for the recurrence, if applicable.
    • onMonday to onSunday: (boolean) Indicates if the schedule runs on the respective day of the week.
    • firstRun: (string) Date and time of the first run in ISO 8601 format.
    • dayCount: (integer) Day on which Monthly recurrence has to run.
  • addStoryLink: (boolean) Enter ‘true’ if you want to add a link to the story and ‘false’ if you do not want to add a link to the story.
  • fileFormat: (string) Enter ‘PDF’ to share a PDF copy of the story or ‘PPT’ to share a PPT format of the story).
  • emailSubject: (string) Subject line for email notifications.
  • emailMessage: (string) Message body for email notifications.
  • deliveryOption: (integer) Specifies the email delivery Option. Enter 0 for Attachment, 1 for Attachment and preview inline, and 2 for Preview Inline
  • timezoneFullName: (string) Full name of the timezone for scheduling.
  • views: (array) List of views to be included in the generated output.
    • bookmarkId: (string) Identifier for the bookmark. Use default “STORY” for scheduling without any bookmark
    • viewName: (string) Name of the view.
    • filename: (string) Name of the file to be generated.
    • fileSettings: (object) Settings for file generation.
      • selectedStoryPages: (array) List of selected pages for the story.
      • includeAppendix: (boolean) Indicates if the appendix should be included.
      • appendixIncludeDate: (boolean) Indicates if the date should be included in the appendix.
      • resolution: (string) Resolution of the output file (e.g., "1920,1080").
      • includeComments: (boolean) Indicates if comments should be included.
      • pageSize: (string) Size of the pages (e.g., "Auto").
    • destinations: (object) Delivery destinations for the file.
      • email: (object) Email delivery settings.
        • extEmailUsr: (array) Enter the email address of the external user/s (ex:abc@gmail.com).
        • internalEmailUsr: (array) Enter the user ID or Team ID of the internal user/s.
  • xsTimeTaken: (integer) Time taken for the execution in milliseconds.

User Authorization through OAuth 3.0 in SAC

Having administrator privileges on our SAC tenant, we can register an OAuth-client in the App Integration tab of the administration page. We navigate to the administration page by clicking on ≡ (Main Menu) -> System -> Administration.

Within the App Integration tab (1), we are now able to register a new OAuth client by clicking on +Add New OAuth Client (2).

This will open the dialog for registering a new OAuth Client as shown below:

Client Configuration Instructions

  1. Client Name: Choose a name for your client.
  2. Purpose: Select “Interactive Usage.”
  3. Redirect URI: Indicate the callback URL that the authorization server will return to after successful authorization.
  4. Token Lifetimes: Defining a lifetime for access and refresh tokens is optional.
  5. Final Steps:
    • Once you provide the required details, ensure to copy the Client ID and Client Secret for use in your application.

Obtaining the Access Token

The diagram below shows the Authorization Code Grant flow for our context and helps you implement it. The end-user authorizes an application to access a protected resource on the Service Provider (SP) and must interact with the IdP (authorization server).

Please note that the SCP Authentication Service is the default IdP but can be changed.

Our custom application should first direct the user to the authorization server's authorization endpoint (Authorization URL). After obtaining the authorization code, the application must exchange it for an access token by making a POST request with the authorization code to the token endpoint (Token URL).

You can find the Authorization URL and Token URL in the App Integration tab under OAuth Clients on SAC's administration page.

Using Postman to Call the Public API

  1. Open Postman and create a new collection.

  2. Go to the Authorization page and fill in the following details to get a new access token:

    • Auth Type: OAuth 2.0
    • Token Name: Provide a suitable token name.
    • Grant Type: Select Authorization Code.
    • Callback URL: Check "Authorize using browser."
    • Auth URL: Use the Authorization URL received from the App Integration page.
    • Client ID: Use the Client ID received while creating the app.
    • Client Secret: Use the client secret obtained in the previous step.
    •   

  3. Click on "Get New Access Token." This will open your browser for user authentication. Once authentication is complete, you will be redirected to Postman with the token.

  4. Use the access token obtained to call the endpoints.

  5. CSRF Token: You will need the CSRF token to call the API endpoints. Use the following code in the Pre-Request script to get and set the csrf_token header:

 

 

 

 

const getAppInstancePropsRequest = {
  url: "https://<tenant-url>/api/v1/csrf",
  method: 'GET',
  header: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' , 'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + pm.collectionVariables.get("bearer.token"), 'x-csrf-token': 'fetch',
  'x-sap-sac-custom-auth': "true" }
};

pm.sendRequest(getAppInstancePropsRequest, function (err, response) {
    console.log(response)
    var csrfToken = response.headers.get("x-csrf-token");
    pm.collectionVariables.set("csrf_token", csrfToken);
})

entry.title: Apply Now for SAP training program at ASUE
entry.content:
The Armenian State University of Economics is launching a special SAP training program for individuals interested in becoming SAP professionals. The program will be led by experts from local SAP partners, customers, and guest lecturers from SAP. 

The course is open to everyone(even for non students), though applicants associated with ASUE, RAU, and TUMO will be given automatic acceptance, as these institutions have the SAP University Alliance membership. If not accepted for this course, your candidacy will be considered for next year’s offerings.

Why Join?

  • No prior SAP experience is required—making it perfect for anyone looking to begin their journey in the SAP world!
  • Participants who successfully complete the course will have the opportunity to receive a free SAP certification attempt.
  • Best performing participants will also have access to 3-month internships at organizations within the local SAP ecosystem. After finishing the internships, the top candidates will be offered full-time job opportunities.

Course Details:

  • Application Deadline: October 27, 2024
  • Cost: Free of charge
  • Duration: The pilot course will run over the next 4 months, with additional SAP courses and bootcamps expected to be held in spring 2024.
  • Start: Beginning of November 2024
  • Weekly study hours:  2-3 hours
  • Prerequisite: English Fluency
  • Topics: SAP S/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Location: Virtual

How to Apply:

Step 1:  Send your CV (and LinkedIn profile, if available) to email address sityerevan@gmail.com. or via https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdr0IU4g1gcTVbF6bRr28ulZAaVh0XxC3CIyB7_Z0uqK7orYA/viewform 

Step 2:  If you are also interested in receiving the free certification attempt, please also apply to the Digital Skills initiative. While there are no specific prerequisites for ASUE course participation except English fluency, those aiming to qualify also for the free certification attempt at the end of this course, must meet one of the criteria listed in the Digital Skills initiative and apply by November 4, 2024, by submitting a comment as described in their respective blog post.

In case of questions please contact sityerevan@gmail.com or sos.khachikyan@gmail.com.  Or leave your questions as comments to this blog post.

Wishing everyone a smooth and successful beginning to their SAP journey!

entry.title: Choosing the Right Path for SAP S/4HANA Transformation: Greenfield, Brownfield, or Bluefield?
entry.content:
transformation involves several key steps

  1. System Conversion: Moving from the old SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA, which requires a technical upgrade and data migration.
  2. New Implementation: Implementing SAP S/4HANA from scratch for organizations starting fresh or restructuring their business processes.
  3. Selective Data Transition: Combining elements of both system conversion and new implementation, selecting specific data or processes to move.

The transformation offers improved performance, real-time analytics, and simplified IT landscapes due to S/4HANA’s in-memory database and modern user experience through Fiori.

 

Greenfield Implementation                  Brownfield Implementation                 Bluefield Implementation

A fresh, new implementation of SAP S/4HANA from scratch.A system conversion that migrates an existing SAP ERP system to S/4HANA.A hybrid approach that blends Greenfield and Brownfield strategies.
Used when starting with new processes or overhauling old systems.Keeps existing customizations, configurations, and processes while upgrading to S/4HANA.Selectively retains and transforms existing processes and data.
Ideal for companies looking for full transformation.Suitable for organizations aiming to keep most of their current system setup.Often used to minimize risk, time, and cost by combining the best of both worlds.

comparing the pros and cons of Greenfield, Brownfield, and Bluefield implementation in SAP:

Approach             Pros                                                                       Cons

Greenfield- Complete process redesign and optimization.
- Full flexibility in implementing best practices.
- No legacy system constraints.		- Time-consuming.
- Higher costs due to full rebuild.
- Requires significant change management.
Brownfield- Faster implementation.
- Retains existing customizations and processes.
- Less disruption to current operations.		- Limited flexibility to adopt new processes.
- Potential migration of inefficiencies.
- May require extensive technical adjustments.
Bluefield- Combines benefits of Greenfield and Brownfield.
- Selective process and data transformation.
- Reduces cost and risk of full rebuild.		- Can be complex to manage.
- Requires careful planning to balance old and new.
- May not achieve full innovation like Greenfield.

 

I tried to keep this article simple and high-level, making it easy for everyone from novices to experts.

entry.title: A Leap Forward in Encryption: TLS 1.3 Support in S/4HANA
entry.content:
TLS is a cryptographic protocol designed to provide secure communication over a computer network. It is used to establish a secure connection between a client and a server to ensure that the data exchanged is private and reliable.

The Benefits of TLS 1.3

The introduction of TLS1.3 in SAP S/4HANA 2023 brings several benefits designed to enhance security and streamline processes:

  1. Simplified and stronger cipher suites: TLS 1.3 removes support for outdated and potentially insecure algorithms and ciphers, focusing on a more streamlined and secure set. This simplification reduces the potential for misconfigurations and vulnerabilities while strengthening the overall security posture of your communications.
  2. Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) always enabled: With PFS enabled, each TLS session has a unique session key which cannot be recovered by learning the private key, ensuring that your session keys will not be compromised even if the server's private key is. In TLS1.3, PFS is always enabled, enhancing the confidentiality of your data.
  3. Simplified key exchange: TLS 1.3 only supports Diffie-Hellman type key exchange algorithms, removing support for RSA key exchange. This simplifies the key exchange process and eliminates potential vulnerabilities associated with RSA key exchange.

How to Configure TLS 1.3 in SAP S/4HANA OnPremise

Before configuring TLS 1.3, please make sure your system fits the version and patch requirements as detailed in SAP Note 3318423.

TLS versions can be configured via profile parameters. The following cipher suites are recommended by SAP for maximum interoperability, activating TLS 1.2 - 1.3 on the server (inbound) side, and TLS 1.0 - 1.3 on the client (outbound) side:

 Parameter NameParameter Value
Server (inbound)ssl/ciphersuites1569:PFS:HIGH::EC_X25519:EC_P256:EC_HIGH
Client (outbound)ssl/client_ciphersuites1174:PFS:HIGH::EC_X25519:EC_P256:EC_HIGH

As a note of caution: If your previous inbound configuration included TLS 1.0 - 1.1, please take a look at SAP Note 3346659 for how to safely restart your system after deactivating TLS 1.0 - 1.1.

If you prefer to have your inbound side open to TLS 1.0 - 1.1 as well as TLS 1.2 - 1.3, you can use the following parameter instead of the above one:

 Parameter NameParameter Value
Server (inbound)ssl/ciphersuites1159:PFS:HIGH::EC_X25519:EC_P256:EC_HIGH

If you would like to restrict your outbound communication to TLS 1.2 and 1.3 only, the following values are recommended:

 Parameter NameParameter Value
Client (outbound)ssl/client_ciphersuites1584:PFS:HIGH::EC_X25519:EC_P256:EC_HIGH

Please note that SAP currently does not recommend setting the cipher suite string parameter to TLS 1.2 and 1.3 for the outbound side, as this may cause interoperability issues when trying to communicate with older servers. Especially older embedded devices often do not support TLS 1.2 and upwards. Therefore, please verify that you have no incompatible servers in your system landscape before making such a change. You can read more about how to safely configure restrictive parameters for your system in SAP Note 3346659.

It is also possible to configure TLS 1.3 for specific clients in transaction sm59. For type G or H destinations, go to the tab "Logon & Security" and add your custom cipher suite string value in the field "TLS Cipher Suites".

This approach is especially recommended if you would like to activate TLS 1.3 systemwide, but have specific servers in your landscape which do not support TLS 1.3 and behave erroneously when a client tries to establish a TLS 1.3 connection. In this case, you can disable TLS 1.3 for these specific servers via the configuration shown in the above image.

Please also see the sm59 documentation for more details.

TLS 1.3 in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

TLS 1.3 is activated by default in all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Web Dispatchers. You can read more about it in this article.

Relevant SAP Notes for Further Information

SAP has released several SAP Notes explaining which software versions, patch levels, and configurations are required to enable TLS 1.3 for SAP S/4HANA:

3318423 - Is TLS 1.3 Supported by SAP Kernel for ABAP and SAP S/4HANA?
3346659 - TLS Ciphersuite String Configuration for ABAP and SAP Kernel-Based Systems

Conclusion

The support of TLS 1.3 is a significant leap forward for SAP S/4HANA 2023. It simplifies configurations, enhances security through stronger cipher suites, and ensures the safety of your communication through PFS and a simplified key exchange.

entry.title: SuccessConnect 2024-Every employee is a success story with SAP BTP
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform at SuccessConnect 2024

Exciting news, demos and information on innovation, integration, extending HR apps, AI, analytics and workforce planning

It’s time to get ready for this year’s SAP SuccessFactors SuccessConnect event!  This year’s sold-out event takes place in Lisbon, Portugal October 28 through the 30th and features more SAP Business Technology Platform-related sessions than ever before – 18 sessions including one special session on workforce planning available for free in the virtual event!

Discover solutions designed to fuel employee and business success and strategies for improving your employee experiences, decisions, and processes.

To help guide attendees in packing their agendas with as much SAP BTP content and information as possible, here’s a guide of what is happening. Add these sessions to your SuccessConnect agenda now!

For more information on this year’s SuccessConnect event, visit https://www.sap.com/events/successconnect.html or to learn more about how SAP Business Technology Platform can help HR visit http://www.sap.com/btp-for-hr .

General and Business Technology Platform

  • Using SAP Integration Suite to connect HR with the business
    Learn how SAP Integration Suite can help you connect HR across the business by integrating SAP SuccessFactors HCM with other SAP and third-party applications. Hear how organizations of all sizes are using this approach to create a single source of people data, enable end-to-end processes, and drive automation and efficiency. SAP Integration Suite 

  • Using SAP Build to extend your SAP SuccessFactors solutions
    Standardized business processes have come a long way, but every company still has a few things it does differently from everybody else. SAP Build solutions can help extend your human capital management solutions to address your specific business needs. See firsthand how to create low-code/no-code apps that boost end-user adoption and efficiency. SAP Build 

  • Road map and vision: Technology Foundation
    Explore the latest innovations and enhancements in the technology foundation of SAP SuccessFactors solutions, including workforce analytics, security, integration, extensibility, and more. Gain insights into what we have planned for our upcoming releases and get your questions answered by our product experts.

  • See how new innovations help you integrate and extend your SAP SuccessFactors solutions
    Discover how our recent innovations around domain-driven APIs and push events can help transform your business operations and enhance the user experience. Learn how you can use these technologies to achieve near real-time integrations with external service providers and streamline your business processes

  • Build trust with security and identity management technologies
    Discover the key technologies and innovations to help you secure your people data. Explore key challenges and learn how to mitigate risk and develop trust with a deep dive into newly available capabilities, including customer-managed keys in the new advanced encryption add-on and enhancements to the role-based permissions tool.

  • Unlock innovation with the generative AI hub
    The new generative AI hub in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad makes it easier for developers to build applications securely and efficiently with access to a broad range of tools and large language models. See how tools for prompt engineering, experimentation, and other AI capabilities can accelerate your development of applications for HR and more.

 

Analytics and Workforce Planning

  • Hands-on-lab: Create compelling report stories in SAP SuccessFactors HCM
    Designed for reporting administrators, this session will help you learn how to use a single, unified reporting approach to tell compelling stories. Get hands-on experience with key capabilities, such as rich visualizations and intelligent data joins, and learn how to use live data from SAP SuccessFactors solutions. This session is intended for intermediate to advanced reporting administrators with a working knowledge of the report center and role-based security permissions. It is for existing customers only. Laptops and scripts will be supplied. Note that registration is required for this session. Space is limited so that speakers have time to answer attendee questions. If a registered session attendee is five minutes late to the session, we will give their seat to the next person waiting in line. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • Road map and vision: Reporting and analytics
    Explore the latest innovations in analytics and the significant benefits they offer for your business. Discover how to make use of these enhancements by finding out more about our new SAP SuccessFactors Analytics solution, report stories within SAP SuccessFactors solutions, and workforce planning in SAP Analytics Cloud for planning. SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • Improving people sustainability with workforce planning and data analytics
    With skill gaps increasing and regulatory mandates growing more complex, CHROs are turning to longer-term people sustainability. Discover how HR leaders can use AI-driven analytics and strategic workforce planning to create a workforce of the future. Gain insights on using skills analysis to inform talent investments and improve workforce planning. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • Get the mot out of workforce planning templates using SAP Analytics Cloud for planning
    SAP Analytics Cloud for planning includes workforce planning templates. Get an overview of the various templates available in the business content network and the planning processes they support. Whether you’re looking to do operational, strategic, or skill-based workforce planning, these insights can help you. SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • Top three ways to create dynamic operational report stories
    How do you best create stories as unfolding narratives for your users to further explore data in more detail? Gain practical tips on how to use report stories within SAP SuccessFactors solutions to create dynamic, interactive stories that let users dive into details as needed. SAP Analytics Cloud 

 

Customer Speaking Sessions

  • How Eurobank uses SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking and SAP BTP to comply with local labor legislation
    To maintain compliance with a new labor law and avoid costly fines, Eurobank chose the SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking solution and SAP Business Technology Platform. Learn how the financial organization accurately tracks employee work hours, shifts, and overtime while linking data in real time to third-party platforms.

  • Unleashing HR insights: Reporting and workforce analytics at Dr. Oetker
    See how reporting in SAP SuccessFactors solutions has transformed Dr. Oetker’s HR reporting landscape and explore real-world use cases in the SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Analytics solution. From overcoming challenges to unlocking transformative insights, discover the power of reporting and analytics capabilities of SAP solutions. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • How agile HR transformation at Volkswagen increased performance and transparency
    Using the “SAFe” framework, Volkswagen implemented SAP SuccessFactors solutions in one of the largest HR transformation programs worldwide. Join this session to learn how the company established a new product organization to increase performance and transparency.

Demo stations

  • DP106: HR analytics and workforce planning area
    Discover how you can improve company performance and decision-making with data-driven people insights, workforce planning tools, and AI-driven recommendations. SAP Analytics Cloud  SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • DP105: System administration and extensibility area
    See how SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) can help you grow your capabilities with third-party integrations and custom extensions featuring a real-world demo of a re-envisioning automated equipment ordering with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central driving real-time triggers to order new hire equipment in SAP Ariba via SAP Integration Suite with automated service ticket create in SAP Service Cloud. SAP Integration Suite  SAP Build

entry.title: Understanding RISE with SAP: Simplifying the Confusion
entry.content:
This article is not for SAP experts but for individuals who are new to this area of knowledge

Nomenclature Confusion
There is confusion in the industry around the terminology, particularly with names being mixed up, such as 'S4 Rise', 'HANA Rise', 'RISE SAP', or other combinations of these terms. However, the correct term is simply 'RISE with SAP.' Now that the naming is clear.

RISE with SAP is Not a New Product Version
It’s a misconception that RISE with SAP is a new product version, like SAP R/1 (1973) the first version, R/2 (1981), R/3 (1992), ECC (2004), or S/4HANA, which was launched in 2015 and remains the latest version of SAP ERP. Product Versions were introduced with different architecture, functionality and features. The evolution of SAP's versions from R/1 to S/4HANA were for technological advancements and changing business needs over time.

Around two years ago, SAP introduced RISE with SAP. (If anyone knows the exact launch date, let me know. I’ll update the article.) But unlike a new product version release, RISE with SAP is a transformation offering. RISE with SAP can be explained as a type of transformation offering that has a bundling of existing SAP tools, assets, solutions, methodology, learning resources, etc. that focus on providing customers with business transformation-as-a-service that comes in 2 packages Premium package and Premium plus package. Probably best comparable to another offering GROW with SAP.

Without getting into what GROW with SAP exactly is, here is a table that helps explain the offering differences.

 

RISE with SAP  GROW with SAP
Targeted at large enterprises needing a full digital transformation package.Focused on small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs) for fast implementation of cloud ERP solutions.
Includes a comprehensive set of tools like ERP, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and business process intelligence.Provides a simplified, quick-to-deploy version of SAP S/4HANA Cloud for smaller companies.
Offers flexibility for customization and integration for complex IT landscapes.Emphasizes rapid cloud adoption with pre-configured best practices.
Aimed at guiding businesses through their unique transformation journey.Designed to help smaller businesses grow efficiently using SAP's cloud solutions.
More expensive and complex, suitable for large-scale operations.More affordable and streamlined for SMBs seeking to scale quickly.

What Exactly is RISE with SAP?
RISE with SAP is designed to help clients transition their existing SAP systems to the cloud, modernize ERP usage, and increase flexibility. Its objective is to upgrade clients’ legacy ERP systems to an AI-enabled cloud ERP. It’s a (BTaaS) Business-transformation-as-a-service offering, meaning businesses receive a tailored path to modernizing their implementation and operations.

RISE with SAP: What's Included?
  • Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA): The core system that manages everything from finance, hr to supply chain.
  • Business Process Transformation: Tools to analyze and optimize business processes, improving efficiency.
  • Technical Migration Support: Services to simplify moving systems to the cloud.
  • Customizable Options: Adaptable features to suit different industries and company sizes.
  • Single Contract: Bundles everything under one contract for easier management and billing.

Why RISE with SAP is Important for Clients
RISE with SAP helps larger organizations move their SAP systems to the cloud by offering an all-in-one package. It simplifies the migration process, making it easier to manage without the need for separate licenses. The solution focuses on six key areas:

  • Cloud ERP
  • Business process optimization
  • Integration and automation
  • Financial management
  • Supplier network
  • Sustainability

This bundled offering reduces complexity and costs, providing a single contract and pricing for the entire transition. RISE with SAP also takes advantage of SAP’s history of simplifying workflows, ensuring that each company’s transition to the cloud is flexible and tailored to its specific needs.

In Summary
RISE with SAP is a comprehensive package offering designed to help clients move to the cloud, improve their processes, and stay competitive in the market.

I wrote this article because clients often ask me ask me about it. I aimed to keep it simple and easy to understand, even for those unfamiliar with the topic. If any enhancements or corrections are needed, feel free to message me privately, and I'll be happy to review and update it as required.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, October 18th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, October 21st.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

If you are looking for Week 1 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 2 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 3 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 3 Contest Activities - SAP Community

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the second week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt is part of week 4. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  This week's Fun Friday activity will be Spooktoberfest to celebrate Halloween. 

List of activities for contest week 4:
